steps.title: Find the verb's subject
steps.content: The blank follows “which,” which refers to “Chicago Midway International Airport.” Because that noun is singular, the verb in the relative clause must also be singular.

steps.title: Choose singular agreement
steps.content: Choice B uses the plural verb “have been.” Choice C uses plural “were,” and Choice D uses plural “are.” The sentence needs the singular present-tense verb in “which is located in Chicago, Illinois.” The answer is A.
